channel.title: Stratezik Blog — research & AEO
channel.description: Original Toronto / GTA research and practical guides on ChatGPT visibility, answer engine optimisation, and local growth from Stratezik.

item.title: How to Appear in ChatGPT's Answers (and Check If You Already Do)
item.description: ChatGPT names real businesses in its answers. Here is how it decides who, why yours may be missing, and a free way to check whether it mentions you.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat
item.category: why isn't my business in ChatGPT
check if ChatGPT mentions my business

item.title: Toronto AI Citation & ChatGPT Ads Tracker
item.description: Monthly Toronto research on AI citations and ChatGPT ads: which assistants recommend local businesses, plus a competitiveness map across 18 industries. Latest edition and archive.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: Toronto AI Citation Tracker: July 2026
item.description: We asked ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Mode, and Claude 50 Toronto buying questions. 89% named a local business; Perplexity trailed at 74% and sent Scarborough to the UK.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: The Toronto Startup Website Audit 2026
item.description: We audited 50 funded Toronto startups on a 20-point AI-search test. 95% of their AEO points come from defaults; 5% deploy FAQ schema. Median: 59/100.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: The Toronto ChatGPT Ads Index
item.description: Which Toronto industries show ChatGPT ads, how competitive they are, and where the open lanes sit. 18 industries mapped, with real CPC and CPM. Updated monthly.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How to Get Your Toronto Business Recommended by ChatGPT (We Tested 90 Buying Questions)
item.description: Two AI assistants, 90 Toronto buying questions. They named a local business 89% of the time but agreed only half the time. How to be the one they pick.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat
item.category: how to show up in ChatGPT
AI search for local business Toronto
get my business recommended by AI

item.title: ChatGPT Is Already Running Ads in Toronto. We Checked 90 Local Buying Questions to See Who's Buying Them.
item.description: We ran 90 Toronto buying questions through ChatGPT. Ads already show on 48%, from 33 advertisers, but only one was a local business. Industry map, opportunity tiers, and what to do first.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat
item.category: should my business advertise on ChatGPT

item.title: The Shift From Old SEO to Agent-Ready SEO in 2026
item.description: How Toronto teams move from keyword-era SEO to agent-ready content: answer-first pages, provenance, structured data, and citations that survive AI summaries.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: AI-Native GTM Part 4: The 2026 Marketing Hire
item.description: Part 4 of the AI-native GTM series: what your 2026 marketing hire should look like, how to test AI fluency, and the three hires to avoid.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: AI-Native GTM Part 1: Build the Function From Day 1
item.description: Part 1 of our AI-native GTM series for Toronto founders: design a marketing function around agents from day one and out-run US-funded rivals.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: AI-Native GTM Part 2: Be Cited by ChatGPT First
item.description: Part 2 of the AI-native GTM series: why Toronto startups should claim AI search visibility now, before US-funded competitors do. Five practical moves.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: AI-Native GTM Part 3: The Agent Stack by Funding Stage
item.description: Part 3 of the AI-native GTM series: which AI tools and agents to build or buy at each funding stage, pre-seed through Series A. No vendor pitch.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: 10 Signs It Is Time to Partner With a Digital Marketing Agency (GTA SMB Scenarios)
item.description: Part 2 of our GTA agency guide: ten triggers shown as real Scarborough, Toronto, and Ontario neighbourhood scenarios, from no-time owners and leaking ad spend to weak local search, stalled growth, and post-pivot resets, with the first moves Stratezik makes.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat
item.category: signs to hire digital marketing agency

item.title: When GTA SMBs Decide It Is Time for a Digital Marketing Agency
item.description: Ten real triggers Scarborough and Toronto SMBs hit before hiring an agency: flat conversions, rising ad waste, Maps gaps, launches, expansion, reviews, bandwidth limits, and seasonal urgency, plus Stratezik audit steps and an RFP checklist.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How Businesses Get Found and Grow in 2026, Part 1: Brand and Positioning
item.description: Positioning before tactics: differentiation that survives the competitor test, voice and visual identity, reputation systems, and trust signals that make SEO, AI, content, and paid work harder together.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How Businesses Get Found and Grow in 2026, Part 2: SEO and Organic Search
item.description: Keyword fanout, answer-first on-page work, technical plumbing, backlink authority, and indexing gates. Why organic search remains the bedrock AI visibility builds on, not a rival to it.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How Businesses Get Found and Grow in 2026, Part 3: AI Search Visibility
item.description: AEO and GEO citations, conversational fanout, schema and crawler access, share-of-voice audits, and how ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini differ. Built on Part 2 SEO authority.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How Businesses Get Found and Grow in 2026, Part 4: Content Strategy
item.description: Founder-led storytelling, educational guides, ethical lead magnets, short-form video transcripts, repurposing ladders, plus how Part 4 locks to brand, SEO, and AI layers before paid acceleration.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How Businesses Get Found and Grow in 2026, Part 5: Paid and Performance
item.description: Why order matters inside the pillar, paid versus organic interplay, disciplined Google Ads, retargeting with Part 4 assets, CAC and ROAS guardrails, funnel honesty, plus Insectica proof and services alignment.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How to Get Your Business Recommended by ChatGPT (2026 Playbook)
item.description: SOCi visibility stats, citation research, OppAlerts correlations, schema + answer-first tactics, crawler checks, GTA measurement habits, plus honest limits on controlling generative mentions.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: ChatGPT Ads in 2026: Setup, Costs, and When to Skip It
item.description: How OpenAI Ads Manager Beta places sponsored recommendations in ChatGPT, what context hints replace keywords, reported pricing bands, geography, category gates, and honest fit notes for Toronto operators.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: Why Service Businesses Lose on Google Maps
item.description: Whitespark 2026 local ranking factors put GBP primary category ahead of reviews for Maps pack influence. What Ontario service operators should fix first: categories, hours, and per-service site depth.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: How We Scaled a Toronto Pest Control Company: The Insectica Story (700+ Leads, Full Breakdown)
item.description: Month-by-month narrative of Insectica Pest Control’s GTA growth: ten ad groups, CPA-first bidding, 700+ paid conversions at ~$43 CPL, organic impressions up 168x, plus charts and infographic.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat

item.title: Answer Engine Optimisation for Toronto Businesses
item.description: Most websites cited in Google's AI-generated answers don't rank in the organic top 10 for that query. What answer engine optimisation means for Toronto businesses.
item.dc:creator: Shah Md. Rifat
